title: Conjecture 2.4
statement_text: Under the assumptions of Theorem 1.2, the conclusion holds together with the further requirement that $\|x_i\| \leq O(1)$ and $\|y_i\| \leq O(1)$ for all $1 \leq i \leq n$.
context_text: Theorem 1.2 establishes a bipartite Johnson-Lindenstrauss reduction to dimension $t = \lfloor C\log(2+\varepsilon^2 n)/\varepsilon^2 \rfloor$ that approximates inner products up to additive error $\varepsilon$, but does not control the norms of the output vectors. The authors conjecture that the output vectors can simultaneously be taken to have bounded Euclidean norm $O(1)$. If true, this would close the gap between the upper and lower bounds in the first bullet of Theorem 2.2 for all $\varepsilon \geq 2/\sqrt{n}$.
notes: PDF source — norm notation $\|\cdot\|$ appears as bare vertical bars in the raw extract; reconstructed from context. The paper proves two partial results supporting the conjecture: the case $t = \Omega(n)$ (i.e., $\varepsilon = \Theta(1/\sqrt{n})$) is established as Theorem 2.5, and a matching bit-count estimate is proved for the column-query variant.
paper_title: Optimal compression of approximate inner products and dimension reduction

title: Larsen–Nelson Question on Improved Johnson-Lindenstrauss Dimension
statement_text: For any $\varepsilon$, can the target dimension in the Johnson-Lindenstrauss Lemma be improved by replacing $\frac{\log n}{\varepsilon^2}$ with $t = \frac{\log(2 + \varepsilon^2 n)}{\varepsilon^2}$?
context_text: Larsen and Nelson [18] asked whether the Johnson-Lindenstrauss dimension bound $\log n / \varepsilon^2$ can be replaced by the smaller quantity $\log(2+\varepsilon^2 n)/\varepsilon^2$ for small $\varepsilon$. This is non-trivial only for $\varepsilon < 1/n^{0.49}$; the present paper's Theorem 1.2 and the lower-bound results in Corollary 2.3 are explicitly motivated by this question.
paper_contribution: Proves a bipartite version (Theorem 1.2): for any $a_1,\ldots,a_n, b_1,\ldots,b_n \in \mathbb{R}^n$ of norm at most 1 and $t = \lfloor C\log(2+\varepsilon^2 n)/\varepsilon^2 \rfloor$, there exist $x_1,\ldots,x_n, y_1,\ldots,y_n \in \mathbb{R}^t$ with $|\langle x_i, y_j\rangle - \langle a_i, b_j\rangle| \leq \varepsilon$ for all $i,j$, computable in randomized polynomial time. Also proves a matching lower bound (Corollary 2.3) showing no embedding into dimension $o(\log(2+\varepsilon^2 n)/\varepsilon^2)$ is possible for any $\varepsilon \geq 2/\sqrt{n}$.
notes: PDF source. The question is stated informally in the introduction without a labelled theorem environment; reference [18] is cited but publication year is not recoverable from the provided text.
paper_title: Optimal compression of approximate inner products and dimension reduction
